Build your tool

Lifecycle PR Review Policy Configuration

Make PR review a lifecycle-start policy, not an implicit routing accident.

Portable Snapshot

Identity and lineage

HTML Snapshot

This standalone HTML is a shareable snapshot and may drift from its canonical origin.

Doc ID doc:living-doc-pr-review-policy-configuration
Title Lifecycle PR Review Policy Configuration
Scope Standalone living-doc harness lifecycle policy, PR-review routing, closure gates, run contracts, dashboard visibility, and standalone proof runs.
Owner / Team Living Docs
Generated 2026-05-12T06:19:39.045Z
Version / Revision draft
Location URL Unknown
Team Unknown
Canonical Origin docs/living-doc-pr-review-policy-configuration.json
Derived From Unknown
Source Coverage Created from the May 11, 2026 session decision that PR-review behavior must be configured when starting the lifecycle controller, with three explicit modes: disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes. GitHub epic #253 and implementation tickets #254-#263 were created from the acceptance criteria before implementation starts.

Objective Implement lifecycle-start policy configuration for the standalone living-doc harness so a run can explicitly choose whether PR review is disabled, always required before closure, or required only when controller evidence shows source/doc-relevant changes. The controller must carry that policy through run config, worker/reviewer contracts, evidence, routing, side-effect gates, closure review, dashboard artifacts, and lifecycle results. When PR review is required by policy, the lifecycle cannot close until a contract-bound pr-review inference unit has inspected the relevant evidence and produced an approved or not-required result. When PR review is disabled, the controller must not invoke pr-review and must not fabricate PR-review evidence for closure.

Success condition A standalone harness run can be started in each PR review policy mode, proves distinct behavior for disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes, preserves the selected policy in lifecycle artifacts and inference-unit contracts, routes to pr-review only when policy requires it, blocks closure when required PR evidence is missing, allows closure when required PR evidence is satisfied, displays the policy state in the dashboard, and proves both PR-enabled and PR-disabled paths through contract tests and standalone lifecycle runs.

Status Snapshot surface2026-05-12T05:42:14Z

Objective shaped

2026-05-12T05:42:14Z

Policy Decisions act2026-05-12T02:53:36.032Z

Policy is configured at lifecycle start

2026-05-11T12:23:03Z
Ground Truth

This prevents post-hoc inference from deciding that review can be skipped when policy says it is mandatory.

The policy must be durable in lifecycle artifacts so later units and dashboards can inspect it.

Policy is not the same as allowed unit types

2026-05-11T12:23:03Z
Ground Truth

A run can allow pr-review without requiring it.

A run cannot require pr-review unless pr-review is allowed.

Required-before-closure is a pre-closure gate

2026-05-12T06:12:40Z
Ground Truth
Tickets
#269#271

Missing PR-review evidence by itself is not enough to select pr-review; the controller first checks whether commit-intent is still required by hard evidence.

When commit evidence is present and sourceFilesChanged is false, a reviewer verdict such as pr-review-gate-missing can select the contract-bound pr-review unit even if the lifecycle classification is still repairable.

The pr-review unit can satisfy the policy gate before closure, but terminal closure remains blocked until acceptance criteria, objective readiness, closure review, and proof routes also agree.

Inference judges review quality

2026-05-11T12:23:03Z
Ground Truth

This preserves intelligence where it belongs while keeping mandatory gates enforceable.

Gate outcomes are first-class controller state

2026-05-12T02:53:36.032Z
Ground Truth
Tickets
#267#266

This prevents the controller from repeatedly invoking the same gate unit after that unit already produced a blocked output contract.

The invariant applies first to pr-review and should generalize to other controller-owned gates such as commit intent or closure review.

A blocked pr-review result must route to continuation/blocker reasoning with the blocked result path, not another identical pr-review unit.

PR Review Policy Modes surface2026-05-11T12:55:00Z

disabled

2026-05-11T12:23:03Z
Specified

required-before-closure

2026-05-11T12:23:03Z
Specified

required-when-source-changes

2026-05-11T12:23:03Z
Specified

Acceptance Criteria surface2026-05-12T02:53:36.032Z

Lifecycle start accepts explicit PR policy

2026-05-11T14:53:09Z
Implemented Focused Proof
Tickets
#254

Objective term a run can explicitly choose whether PR review is disabled, always required before closure, or required only when controller evidence shows source/doc-relevant changes

Criterion The lifecycle controller accepts a first-class prReviewPolicy at run start with exactly the supported modes disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes.

Proof required CLI/API/run config artifact shows prReviewPolicy as explicit lifecycle policy and rejects unknown modes.

Acceptance test Contract tests prove valid modes parse and invalid modes fail before the first worker starts.

Policy is separate from unit availability

2026-05-11T14:53:09Z
Implemented Focused Proof
Tickets
#255

Objective term policy configuration for the standalone living-doc harness

Criterion allowedUnitTypes and prReviewPolicy are separate run configuration fields with separate validation semantics.

Proof required Tests show pr-review can be allowed but not required, and that requiring pr-review fails when pr-review is not allowed.

Acceptance test A disabled run with pr-review allowed never invokes pr-review; a required run without pr-review allowed fails configuration validation.

Disabled mode never invokes PR review

2026-05-11T14:53:09Z
Implemented Focused Proof
Tickets
#256

Objective term When PR review is disabled, the controller must not invoke pr-review and must not fabricate PR-review evidence for closure.

Criterion In disabled mode, the controller never selects pr-review and closure-review receives policy evidence showing PR review is disabled.

Proof required Lifecycle selection artifacts, graph artifacts, and closure-review input contain no selected pr-review unit and no fake PR side-effect evidence.

Acceptance test A disabled-mode lifecycle can close through normal gates without any pr-review unit in the graph.

Required-before-closure blocks closure until PR evidence exists

2026-05-12T06:12:40Z
Implemented Awaiting Standalone Proof
Tickets
#257#269#271

Objective term When PR review is required by policy, the lifecycle cannot close until a contract-bound pr-review inference unit has inspected the relevant evidence and produced an approved or not-required result.

Criterion In required-before-closure mode, the controller routes missing commit evidence to commit-intent first. After commit evidence is present and the PR gate is still missing, reviewer evidence can select the contract-bound pr-review unit before closure review, while terminal closure remains impossible until the PR gate and all objective proof gates are satisfied.

Proof required Post-review selection and lifecycle graph show commit-intent selected only when hard facts say commit evidence is missing, then pr-review selected when commit evidence is satisfied and prReviewGate.status is missing; closure-review rejects missing PR evidence.

Acceptance test A required-before-closure contract test proves repairable + missing PR evidence + satisfied commit evidence routes to pr-review; separate tests prove current-run source changes without commit evidence still route to commit-intent, blocked pr-review routes to continuation, and approved or not-required PR output can proceed to closure-review.

Required-when-source-changes follows controller hard facts

2026-05-11T14:53:09Z
Implemented Focused Proof
Tickets
#258

Objective term required only when controller evidence shows source/doc-relevant changes

Criterion In required-when-source-changes mode, the controller invokes pr-review only when controller-owned hard facts show source/doc-relevant changes.

Proof required Controller evidence snapshot records the change facts used to require or skip PR review.

Acceptance test Two tests prove distinct behavior: changed source/doc files require pr-review; no relevant changes skip pr-review.

PR review receives a complete contract

2026-05-11T14:53:09Z
Implemented Focused Proof
Tickets
#259

Objective term contract-bound pr-review inference unit has inspected the relevant evidence

Criterion The worker/reviewer contracts carry run policy, and the pr-review unit input contract includes the policy mode, living doc path, evidence snapshot path, reviewer verdict path, changed files or commit evidence when applicable, required hard facts, and required inspection paths.

Proof required Worker, reviewer, and pr-review input-contract.json and validation.json artifacts prove all policy fields are present.

Acceptance test Contract tests reject pr-review input missing policy, evidence snapshot, reviewer verdict, or required inspection paths.

Closure review enforces PR policy

2026-05-11T14:53:09Z
Implemented Focused Proof
Tickets
#260

Objective term The controller must carry that policy through run config, worker/reviewer contracts, evidence, routing, side-effect gates, closure review, dashboard artifacts, and lifecycle results.

Criterion Closure-review input and result validation include PR policy state and reject terminal closure when policy-required PR evidence is absent.

Proof required Closure-review input contract includes prReviewPolicy and prReviewRequired, plus sideEffectEvidence.prReview or a valid not-required result when needed.

Acceptance test Closure-review tests prove required PR evidence blocks closure and satisfied PR evidence allows closure.

Dashboard exposes policy state

2026-05-11T14:53:09Z
Implemented Focused Proof
Tickets
#261

Objective term dashboard artifacts

Criterion The dashboard shows the selected PR policy mode and the PR gate state for each lifecycle run.

Proof required Dashboard graph API and UI inspector expose disabled, required, satisfied, missing, or blocked PR gate state from lifecycle artifacts.

Acceptance test Dashboard contract or E2E test opens the lifecycle graph and verifies policy mode and PR gate status are visible.

Contract tests cover all policy modes

2026-05-11T14:53:09Z
Complete
Tickets
#262

Objective term proves distinct behavior for disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes

Criterion The contract suite covers each mode, invalid configuration, missing PR evidence, satisfied PR evidence, and required policy with pr-review not allowed.

Proof required npm run test:contract passes with focused PR policy assertions.

Acceptance test The focused contract tests fail if any mode collapses into another mode's behavior.

Standalone runs prove enabled and disabled paths

2026-05-11T14:53:09Z
Blocked For Controller Owned Proof
Tickets
#263

Objective term proves both PR-enabled and PR-disabled paths through contract tests and standalone lifecycle runs

Criterion At least one standalone run proves a PR-required path and one standalone run proves a PR-disabled path from lifecycle-result.json, graph artifacts, inference-unit contracts, and closure-review evidence.

Proof required Two .living-doc-runs lifecycle-result.json artifacts show closed final states with the expected presence or absence of pr-review units.

Acceptance test The living doc records the run IDs and proof paths before the objective can be marked closed.

Gate outcome state prevents repeated gate loops

2026-05-12T03:24:52Z
Implemented Awaiting Standalone Proof
Tickets
#267#266

Objective term The controller must carry policy through routing, side-effect gates, closure review, dashboard artifacts, and lifecycle results.

Criterion The controller represents each policy/side-effect gate as missing, requested, satisfied, or blocked, and does not select the same gate unit again for a blocked gate unless new evidence changes the gate state.

Proof required Controller evidence, post-review selection, output-input contracts, and dashboard graph metadata expose prReviewGate.status and preserve blocked pr-review result paths as evidence.

Acceptance test A lifecycle with a blocked pr-review output contract routes to continuation-inference or an explicit blocked state, not another pr-review unit; dashboard graph shows the blocked gate and result artifact.

Relationship Maps surface2026-05-12T03:58:07Z

Policy vs inference judgment

2026-05-11T12:23:03Z
Specified
Sections Policy Decisions Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria criterion-policy-config-surface criterion-required-before-closure criterion-pr-review-contract criterion-closure-enforcement
Invariants policy-decides-mandatory-review required-review-blocks-closure

Relationship claim Policy decides whether PR review is mandatory; inference decides whether the work passes PR review; the controller enforces both through contracts.

Semantic role Normative control model

Validation question Can the run prove that mandatory review came from lifecycle policy and review quality came from the pr-review inference unit?

Drift risk The map is stale if reviewer or closure-review can skip a configured mandatory PR review by semantic judgment alone.

Mermaid diagrams (1)

Policy and Judgment Boundary

Mermaid source
flowchart LR
  Start["Lifecycle start"] --> Policy["prReviewPolicy"]
  Policy --> Controller["Deterministic controller"]
  Evidence["Controller hard facts"] --> Controller
  Controller -->|policy requires review| PRUnit["pr-review inference unit"]
  PRUnit --> ReviewResult["approved / changes-requested / blocked / not-required"]
  ReviewResult --> Controller
  Controller --> ClosureReview["closure-review"]
  Controller -->|policy disabled| ClosureReview
  ClosureReview --> Terminal["terminal closure only if policy gate satisfied"]

Three PR policy modes

2026-05-12T03:58:07Z
Specified
Sections PR Review Policy Modes Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance criteria criterion-disabled-mode criterion-required-before-closure criterion-required-when-source-changes
Invariants allowed-types-do-not-imply-required-types disabled-means-never-invoked

Relationship claim The three modes are distinct lifecycle behaviors, not aliases for the same routing flag.

Semantic role Execution-guiding

Validation question Do tests and lifecycle artifacts prove distinct route behavior for disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes?

Drift risk The map is stale if the system treats allowed pr-review as required, treats required-before-closure as a repair-loop gate, or treats required-when-source-changes as always required.

Mermaid diagrams (1)

Mode Routing

Mermaid source
flowchart TD
  Mode["prReviewPolicy.mode"] --> Disabled["disabled"]
  Mode --> Always["required-before-closure"]
  Mode --> Changed["required-when-source-changes"]
  Disabled --> NoPR["Never select pr-review"]
  Always --> CommitGate{"commit evidence missing?"}
  CommitGate -->|yes| CommitIntent["commit-intent contract"]
  CommitGate -->|no and PR gate missing| NeedPR["Select pr-review"]
  CommitIntent --> CommitGate
  Changed --> Facts{"source/doc-relevant changes?"}
  Facts -->|yes and PR gate missing| NeedPR
  Facts -->|no| SkipPR["Skip pr-review"]
  NoPR --> Closure["Normal closure-review gates"]
  NeedPR --> PRUnit["pr-review contract"]
  PRUnit --> Closure
  SkipPR --> Closure

Gate outcome state machine

2026-05-12T02:53:36.032Z
Newly Scoped
Sections Policy Decisions Acceptance Criteria Verification Plan
Acceptance criteria criterion-gate-outcome-state-model criterion-required-before-closure criterion-dashboard-policy-visibility
Invariants blocked-gate-is-real-evidence no-repeat-same-gate-without-new-evidence
Tickets
#267#266

Relationship claim A controller-owned gate is not binary. Missing, requested, satisfied, and blocked are distinct states with different next-unit routing obligations.

Semantic role Control invariant

Validation question Can the lifecycle prove that a blocked pr-review output becomes blocked gate evidence and cannot immediately select pr-review again without new evidence?

Drift risk The map is stale if blocked PR-review output is treated as absent evidence or if the controller repeats the same gate unit for the same blocked state.

Mermaid diagrams (2)

Gate Outcome RoutingstateDiagram-v2[*] --> MissingMissing --> Requested: controller selects gate unitRequested --> Satisfied: unit returns approved/not-requiredRequested --> Blocked: unit returns blockedSatisfied --> ClosureReview: gate can support closureBlocked --> Continuation: explain blocker and choose non-repeating next moveBlocked --> Requested: only if new evidence changes gate stateContinuation --> [*]

PR Review Loop Guard

Mermaid source
flowchart LR
  Reviewer["reviewer: pr-review-policy-gate-missing"] --> Controller["controller selection"]
  Controller --> PR["pr-review unit"]
  PR --> Blocked["outputContract.status = blocked"]
  Blocked --> Evidence["prReviewGate.status = blocked"]
  Evidence --> Guard{"new evidence changed gate?"}
  Guard -->|no| Continue["continuation-inference / blocker"]
  Guard -->|yes| PR
  Continue --> Dashboard["dashboard shows blocked gate + resultPath"]

Verification Plan surface2026-05-12T04:01:52Z

Contract suite

2026-05-12T06:12:40Z
Complete Awaiting Standalone Proof

Current coverage npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-12T06:12:40.000Z. Focused contract tests now cover policy validation, separated allowed-unit semantics, iteration PR gates, lifecycle controller routing, policy propagation, closure blocking/satisfaction, dashboard graph metadata, source-change-gated PR routing in both directions, URL-only or result-path-only PR metadata rejection, closure-review inspection of the satisfying PR-review output contract path, invalid policy mode rejection, dashboard lifecycle-start API propagation, lifecycle result path references for runs created outside the repo, the gate outcome loop guard where blocked pr-review output routes to continuation-inference instead of another pr-review, and the post-commit policy path where satisfied commit evidence plus missing required PR evidence selects pr-review instead of repeating commit-intent.

Next step Record the controller-owned standalone lifecycle proof routes, including proof that required-before-closure advances from commit-intent evidence to pr-review and that disabled mode never invokes pr-review.

Current gaps (1)

Standalone lifecycle proof remains controller-owned.

Standalone lifecycle proof

2026-05-12T03:58:07Z
Blocked For Controller Owned Proof
Tickets
#269

Current coverage Implementation and focused fixture runs prove routing behavior, including closure-boundary PR-review routing, but controller-owned standalone proof still has to validate the behavior in a full lifecycle.

Next step The lifecycle controller must run this living doc through the harness and record both PR-enabled and PR-disabled lifecycle results. The PR-enabled run must prove PR-review is not invoked while the doc remains repairable/resumable and is invoked only when closure is otherwise available.

Current gaps (3)

Standalone required-before-closure run artifact is not recorded yet.

Standalone disabled run artifact is not recorded yet.

Standalone closure-boundary PR-review routing proof is not recorded yet.

Focused E2e Pass

The focused Playwright E2E passed on 2026-05-12T02:18:30.000Z for the policy routing and inference-unit routing dashboard paths.

Next step Confirm dashboard artifacts from the controller-owned standalone lifecycle runs.

Current gaps (1)

Controller-owned standalone dashboard artifact is not recorded yet.

Gate outcome loop guard

2026-05-12T03:24:52Z
Implemented Awaiting Standalone Proof
Tickets
#267#266

Current coverage Controller evidence now carries prReviewGate.status, sideEffectEvidenceFromRun preserves blocked pr-review output contracts with resultPath, validationPath, reasonCode, status, and basis, and post-review selection routes a blocked pr-review gate to continuation-inference instead of repeating pr-review. Focused contract tests passed for iteration routing, lifecycle controller ingestion, and dashboard artifact graph exposure.

Next step Rerun the PR-enabled standalone lifecycle from this living doc and confirm that the blocked pr-review case no longer launches repeated pr-review units for the same gate state.

Current gaps (2)

Standalone lifecycle proof has not yet rerun after the gate outcome implementation.

Dashboard confirmation from a live controller-owned run still needs to be recorded.

Scope Boundaries act2026-05-11T12:23:03Z

No full GitHub PR automation in this slice

2026-05-11T12:23:03Z
Out Of Scope

This objective is about lifecycle policy, routing, contracts, and closure enforcement.

No deep PR review rubric in this slice

2026-05-11T12:23:03Z
Out Of Scope

The pr-review unit must receive the right evidence and produce a valid contract, but improving its semantic rubric can be a later objective.
